title: Percentage Decrease Calculator
shortDescription: Find the new value after a percentage decrease by entering the starting amount and the decrease rate.
metaDescription: Find the new value after a percentage decrease by entering the starting amount and the decrease rate.
intro: Enter the original amount and the percentage decrease to get the reduced value immediately.
overview:
This calculator gives the final value after a percentage reduction, so you can see the exact amount left after a drop. It is useful whenever a price, budget, target, quantity, or measurement needs to be reduced by a known percentage. The result is calculated from the starting value and the decrease rate, which helps avoid mistakes when doing the math by hand.

If you are comparing two numbers, planning a markdown, checking a funding cut, or working out a smaller target after a reduction, the tool gives you the revised figure directly. It also makes it easy to test different decrease rates and see how much remains after each change.
howItWorks: Get the reduced final value and adjust the inputs if you want to compare another scenario.

useCases.title: Retail markdowns
useCases.description: Work out the new shelf price after a seasonal discount or clearance reduction.

useCases.title: Budget revisions
useCases.description: Calculate how much remains after a department budget or project allowance is cut.

useCases.title: Performance drops
useCases.description: Measure the new level after a decline in revenue, sign-ups, output, or other tracked figures.

useCases.title: Target adjustment
useCases.description: Reduce a sales, savings, or production target by a fixed percentage and see the new goal.

examples.title: Store discount
examples.description: A product priced at 84 becomes 63 after a 25% reduction.

examples.title: Rent reduction
examples.description: A monthly amount of 1,600 falls to 1,480 after the decrease.

examples.title: Lead count drop
examples.description: A count of 2,400 is reduced to 2,112 when the decrease is applied.

faq.question: How do I read the result?
faq.answer: The result is the original value minus the chosen percentage of that original value.

faq.question: Can this tool handle decimal percentages?
faq.answer: Yes. You can enter decimal rates such as 7.5% or 12.25% and the calculator will apply them to the starting value.

faq.question: Why is the result sometimes a decimal number?
faq.answer: If the decrease does not divide evenly into the original value, the final amount may include decimals.

faq.question: What mistake should I avoid when using it?
faq.answer: Do not enter the already reduced value as the starting amount, or the result will be reduced twice.
